title: Gorgeous Grampa

jokes.text: Writing is for bathroom walls, and acting is for getting out of DUIs.
jokes.setup: Discussion about the golden age of well-written TV shows
jokes.punchline: Homer's crude dismissal of writing and acting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer dismissal of high culture with crude but specific observations. Strong economy and character voice.

jokes.text: The only reason TV exists is for reality shows about white-trash scuzzbags doing subhuman, made-up jobs.
jokes.setup: Homer's anti-intellectual stance on television
jokes.punchline: Cynical but accurate description of reality TV
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary disguised as Homer's crude observation. Gains on rewatch as reality TV landscape proves him right.

jokes.text: Um... Oh. Ah. Eek. Yikes. Oh. Ew. Ugh. Well... Ew. Ah. Dad! No. Ah! Oh. Seriously? Ah! Ooh, jail music!
jokes.setup: Lisa watching reality TV with mounting horror
jokes.punchline: Rapid-fire disgust responses culminating in 'jail music'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa's intellectual horror at reality TV. 'Jail music' is a nice specific detail.

jokes.text: If there's wrap, it ain't crap.
jokes.setup: Reality show contestant finding bubble wrap in storage unit
jokes.punchline: Rhyming appraisal philosophy
jokes.explanation: Catchy rhyme that parodies reality show expertise. Simple but effective wordplay.

jokes.text: Five hundaminidos!
jokes.setup: Excitement over $500 valuation
jokes.punchline: Made-up Spanish-inflected celebration
jokes.explanation: Silly made-up word that captures reality show host enthusiasm. Mildly amusing nonsense.

jokes.text: I want to know what it's for. Too late.
jokes.setup: Homer demanding high-five before explaining plan
jokes.punchline: Homer's complete lack of consideration for Marge's reasonable request
jokes.explanation: Perfect married couple dynamic. Homer's selfishness is both funny and true to character.

jokes.text: I bet I win a locker full of treasure, like a solid gold bar of silver or Picasso's TV.
jokes.setup: Homer fantasizing about treasure
jokes.punchline: Contradictory precious metal and anachronistic art reference
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer logic failure. 'Solid gold bar of silver' and 'Picasso's TV' are perfectly stupid contradictions.

jokes.text: Oh, no, no, no. It is I who will be watching Picasso's TV tonight!
jokes.setup: Ned overhearing Homer's treasure fantasy
jokes.punchline: Ned's formal speech pattern applied to Homer's absurd concept
jokes.explanation: Good callback that works because Ned's formal manner makes the absurd concept funnier.

jokes.text: There's other P.O.'s?!
jokes.setup: Learning about other 'predatory opportunists'
jokes.punchline: Homer's shock at competition, abbreviated as 'P.O.'s'
jokes.explanation: Nice abbreviation joke and Homer's naive assumption he's unique in his schemes.

jokes.text: I wasn't laughing, I was being worried.
jokes.setup: Homer saying Marge won't be laughing when boxes are full of treasure
jokes.punchline: Marge's literal correction about her emotional state
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge response - deadpan literal correction that reveals her perpetual anxiety about Homer's schemes.

jokes.text: I think your dad might be gay.
jokes.setup: Finding feminine items in Grampa's storage locker
jokes.punchline: Marge's matter-of-fact conclusion
jokes.explanation: Sets up the episode's central misunderstanding. Marge's direct delivery is funny but the concept is fairly predictable.

jokes.text: My dad was married to a woman who left him because he ignored her needs for decades.
jokes.setup: Homer defending Grampa's heterosexuality
jokes.punchline: Accidentally describing a terrible marriage as proof of straightness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant accidental self-own. Homer thinks he's defending his dad but actually describes emotional neglect. Dark comedy gold.

jokes.text: Old gay men are adorable. Like wrinkle dogs in a wrinkle dog calendar.
jokes.setup: Marge expressing support for gay Grampa
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific comparison to hypothetical wrinkle dog calendar
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific and weird comparison. 'Wrinkle dog calendar' is an inspired bit of absurdist detail.

jokes.text: They even had a gay float in the Pride Parade last year.
jokes.setup: Marge explaining society's tolerance
jokes.punchline: Redundant description of Pride Parade having gay representation
jokes.explanation: Marge's well-meaning but redundant observation. The joke is her not understanding what Pride Parades are for.

jokes.text: This'll show Helen Lovejoy. Always bragging about her he-she cousin.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming noble motives for supporting gay rights
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's actually about petty competition with neighbor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character revelation. Subverts his seemingly progressive stance to reveal petty social competition. Excellent craft.

jokes.text: I was just taking Stanlerina to get her hormone shots.
jokes.setup: Patty walking a small dog
jokes.punchline: Implying the dog is transgender and getting hormone therapy
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation of pet pampering. The name 'Stanlerina' suggests transition, fitting Patty's character.

jokes.text: It's weird that you don't consider yourself a young person. I know. It's weird to me, too.
jokes.setup: Discussion about how young people date online
jokes.punchline: Marge's acceptance of being middle-aged
jokes.explanation: Relatable aging humor. Marge's resigned acceptance of not being young anymore hits home for adult viewers.

jokes.text: If I can't find my father a man to kiss and cuddle, then I've failed as a son.
jokes.setup: Homer's mission to help his supposedly gay father
jokes.punchline: Defining filial success in terms of finding dad a boyfriend
jokes.explanation: Homer's bizarre metric for parental success. The specificity of 'kiss and cuddle' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I don't ride sidesaddle! I'm straight as a submarine!
jokes.setup: Grampa denying he's gay
jokes.punchline: Mixed metaphor comparing straightness to submarines (which dive)
jokes.explanation: Brilliant malapropism. Submarines aren't straight at all - they dive and maneuver. Perfect Grampa confusion with great quotability.

jokes.text: I so wanted him to be gay.
jokes.setup: Learning Grampa was actually a famous wrestler, not gay
jokes.punchline: Homer's disappointed reaction to his father being straight
jokes.explanation: Perfectly delivered disappointment. Homer's genuine sadness that his dad isn't gay is both sweet and absurd.

jokes.text: Well, on V.E. Day, I kissed a man by mistake. Thank you.
jokes.setup: Homer desperately wanting Grampa to be gay
jokes.punchline: Grampa throwing Homer a bone with wartime confusion, Homer's grateful response
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment. Grampa indulging Homer's wishes, and Homer's pathetic gratitude for any crumb of gayness.

jokes.text: Word beans!
jokes.setup: Abe says he'll 'spill the beans'
jokes.punchline: Homer enthusiastically mishears/misunderstands as 'word beans'
jokes.explanation: Homer's characteristic misunderstanding of idioms, creating new nonsensical phrase

jokes.text: the manly mixture of strong and fat we called beefy
jokes.setup: Describing 1950s wrestling physiques
jokes.punchline: Defining 'beefy' as combination of strong and fat
jokes.explanation: Good period humor. The phrase 'manly mixture of strong and fat' perfectly captures old-school wrestling body types.

jokes.text: How dare he! That was drinking milk!
jokes.setup: Wrestling flashback showing Grampa as villain
jokes.punchline: Crowd outraged by the wholesome act of drinking milk
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor. Being outraged by milk consumption perfectly captures the arbitrary nature of wrestling heel heat.

jokes.text: You are the luckiest people in the world. You get to look at me.
jokes.setup: Glamorous Godfrey's heel character
jokes.punchline: Ultimate narcissistic statement to wrestling crowd
jokes.explanation: Perfect heel line. Becomes a running gag and perfectly captures narcissistic villain mentality. Highly quotable and reusable.

jokes.text: I was boastful before all your football showboaters, rap music-ers and TV beach Italians.
jokes.setup: Grampa claiming to be original bad boy
jokes.punchline: Listing modern showboats with old-man terminology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Grampa voice. 'Rap music-ers' and 'TV beach Italians' are wonderfully specific old-man descriptions.

jokes.text: Pshaw! Pshaw, I say! Pshaw yourself! Pshaw! Pshaw!
jokes.setup: Wrestling crowd getting worked up
jokes.punchline: Escalating exchange of old-fashioned exclamations
jokes.explanation: Mild escalation gag using antiquated exclamation. The build is okay but not particularly inspired.

jokes.text: that conceited mother-puncher was my hero
jokes.setup: Burns describing young fascination with villain wrestler
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic swearing appropriate to character and era
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Burns. 'Mother-puncher' is appropriately old-fashioned censored swearing.

jokes.text: Should I really let the men who ruined my past and present hang out with the kid who's going to ruin my future?
jokes.setup: Grampa considering dinner with Homer and Burns
jokes.punchline: Categorizing family members by how they've ruined his life across time periods
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy. Perfect structure organizing family disappointment across past, present, and future. Highly quotable cynicism.

jokes.text: That cocaine I ordered is taking forever!
jokes.setup: Burns at restaurant getting impatient
jokes.punchline: Casual drug reference as restaurant complaint
jokes.explanation: Good Burns moment treating illegal drugs like a normal restaurant order. The casualness makes it funnier.

jokes.text: He's always pretended to be boring, but he's really a straight-up psycho.
jokes.setup: Bart's admiration for Grampa's wrestling persona
jokes.punchline: Describing boring grandfather as secret psychopath
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Bart's admiration of rebellious behavior. The contrast between boring and psycho is effective.

jokes.text: Folding chairs! Folding chairs! Can't hit somebody when they're not looking without a folding chair!
jokes.setup: Wrestling arena vendor selling concessions
jokes.punchline: Selling weapons for wrestling interference as normal concessions
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist concept - selling wrestling weapons like hot dogs. The vendor's enthusiasm for violence is perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: You are the luckiest people in the world! You get to look at me!
jokes.setup: Grampa back in wrestling ring as Glamorous Godfrey
jokes.punchline: Repeating his signature narcissistic catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Effective callback that works even better in context. The line gains power through repetition and situation.

jokes.text: Why do you still have a thing for Nelson?
jokes.setup: Discussion about why bad guys are cool
jokes.punchline: Lisa calling out Bart's crush on the school bully
jokes.explanation: Good character insight. Lisa connecting Bart's attraction to villains with his confusing relationship with Nelson.

jokes.text: He's a rebel, and only I can change him!
jokes.setup: Bart denying his thing for Nelson
jokes.punchline: Immediately confirming it with classic relationship delusion
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline execution. Bart immediately contradicts his denial with classic 'I can fix him' mentality.

jokes.text: Why do you think bad guys are so cool? Why do you still have a thing for Nelson?
jokes.setup: Lisa questions Bart's admiration for villains
jokes.punchline: Bart counters by calling out Lisa's attraction to bad boy Nelson
jokes.explanation: Perfect sibling comeback that reveals Lisa's own contradiction about bad boys

jokes.text: And I've worked with Bob Balaban!
jokes.setup: Director complaining about Bart's self-regard
jokes.punchline: Citing working with famously neurotic character actor as expertise in difficult personalities
jokes.explanation: Oddly specific Hollywood reference. Bob Balaban is a perfect choice for someone difficult to work with.

jokes.text: I got one just for watching the game.
jokes.setup: Bart getting participant trophy
jokes.punchline: Homer getting trophy for even less participation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke about participation trophy culture. Homer getting rewarded for pure spectatorship is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: if he wasn't a terrible father, what's my excuse for this?
jokes.setup: Homer defending Grampa's influence on Bart
jokes.punchline: Homer admitting he needs Grampa to be bad to excuse his own failures
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark character moment. Homer admitting he needs someone else to blame for his parenting failures. Excellent self-awareness.

jokes.text: If professional wrestling were fake, that would make every fan in the history of the sport a complete and utter moron.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming wrestling is fake
jokes.punchline: Grampa's logic that it can't be fake because that would make fans idiots
jokes.explanation: Good circular logic joke. Grampa's reasoning is flawed but understandable - he can't accept wrestling fans are fools.

jokes.text: he ain't beefy, but he is infuriating, and you can't teach infuriating.
jokes.setup: Burns evaluating Bart's wrestling potential
jokes.punchline: Identifying natural talent for being annoying
jokes.explanation: Great line about Bart's natural abilities. 'You can't teach infuriating' is a perfect assessment of his talents.

jokes.text: Eye gouge! Ear bite! Folding chair! Hair pull! Face-fart! Flying face-fart! And then I start fighting dirty.
jokes.setup: Bart listing his wrestling moves
jokes.punchline: Claiming these outrageous moves are his clean fighting before getting dirty
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation ending with the punchline that these ARE his clean moves. 'Flying face-fart' is wonderfully specific.

jokes.text: The world is my ring now.
jokes.setup: Bart being asked to save bad attitude for the wrestling ring
jokes.punchline: Bart declaring everywhere is now his wrestling ring
jokes.explanation: Good character development showing Bart's attitude spreading beyond wrestling. Ominous and economical delivery.

jokes.text: Curse you, and this morbidly obese little half-wit.
jokes.setup: Burns angry about Grampa becoming a good guy
jokes.punchline: Burns' elaborate insult toward Bart
jokes.explanation: Standard Burns insult with his characteristic verbose cruelty. 'Morbidly obese little half-wit' is appropriately mean.

jokes.text: This is why we go to everything in this town-- it always pays off.
jokes.setup: Wrestling match turning into exciting spectacle
jokes.punchline: Marge's meta-commentary on Springfield events always becoming entertaining
jokes.explanation: Nice meta joke about The Simpsons' formula. Marge acknowledging that Springfield events always escalate into entertainment.

jokes.text: This is why we go to everything in this town-- it always pays off.
jokes.setup: Another Springfield event turns unexpectedly dramatic
jokes.punchline: Marge recognizes pattern of Springfield events becoming chaotic entertainment
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta-commentary on The Simpsons' format where routine events become spectacles

jokes.text: I wish Grampa was my dad.
jokes.setup: Grampa being cheered as a good guy wrestler
jokes.punchline: Bart preferring his grandfather over his father
jokes.explanation: Sweet ending moment showing Bart's admiration for Grampa's newfound heroism. Good character beat but not particularly funny.

jokes.text: Damndest boos I ever heard. They're cheering, Grampa.
jokes.setup: Abe confused by crowd reaction
jokes.punchline: Abe thinks cheering sounds like unusual booing
jokes.explanation: Sweet moment of Abe's unfamiliarity with positive crowd reaction after lifetime of being villain

jokes.text: I wish Grampa was my dad.
jokes.setup: Touching moment between Abe and Bart
jokes.punchline: Bart wishes for better paternal figure than Homer
jokes.explanation: Touching but somewhat predictable moment of Bart preferring Abe to Homer

tmdb_overview: Homer's obsession with the TV show "Storage Battles" leads the family to discover Grampa's past identity as "Gorgeous Godfrey," the most feared, villainous man in pro-wrestling history
